t_ch: 已经…联系不上云骑主力了。
t_en: It's already... impossible to contact the main Cloud Knight forces.

t_ch: 接下来，将是漫长而孤独的战斗。
t_en: What awaits me will be a long and lonely war.

t_ch: 我们已经反抗过一次又一次……
t_en: We've resisted time and time again...

t_ch: 世界的命运掌握在他们自己手中。
t_en: The destiny of this world rests in their own hands.

t_ch: 他们已经掌握了名为反抗的力量。
t_en: They have already gained the power of resistance.

t_ch: 这个世界将只剩下唯一的怪物……
t_en: Soon, this world will only be left with one final monster...

t_ch: …谢谢你，老友。
t_en: ...Thank you, my old friend.

t_ch: 到底还是没能回到仙舟罗浮……
t_en: I never made it back to the Xianzhou Luofu...

t_ch: 我不想成为一柄杀人的剑。
t_en: I don't want to be a sword that brings death.

t_ch: 属于我的战争早已经结束了。
t_en: My war ended long ago.

t_ch: 作为墓碑的岁月，是我最好的岁月。
t_en: The years spent as a tombstone were my finest.

t_ch: 有些伤感…我想在这儿待一阵。
t_en: I'm feeling a bit emotional... I'd like to stay here for a while.

t_ch: 这一路上谢谢你的劝诫，{NICKNAME}。无以为报…不知道你是不是有兴趣和三月一起在我这儿学剑？
t_en: Thank you for your guidance, {NICKNAME}. I'm truly grateful... By any chance, would you be interested in taking sword lessons with me, like March?

t_ch: 不了吧，我有球棒…
t_en: Maybe not, I have my baseball bat...

t_ch: 算了算了，我怕自己吃不起苦。
t_en: Thanks, but no thanks. I don't think I can endure the hardship.

t_ch: 我姑且算是「盗剑从犯」——怀炎将军让我在这金色树苗旁罚站，向往来行人传诵英雄和这柄剑的故事。
t_en: As a punishment for being an "accomplice" in the theft, General Huaiyan assigned me to guard this golden sapling and recite the story of the hero and this legendary sword to passers-by.

t_ch: 这哪里算是惩罚，我欣然接受！向仙舟住民传递纯美的信念，正是在下所求。
t_en: This is not a punishment at all, and I gladly accepted it! Spreading the message of Beauty to the Xianzhou residents is exactly what I desire.

t_ch: 不过，再过一段时间，我打算离开仙舟，继续旅行。希望我们有缘在银河相会吧，挚友。
t_en: However, I plan to leave the Xianzhou soon and continue my travels. May our paths cross again in the vast Cosmos, my dear friend.

t_ch: 完成植树使命之后，我们使团众人也将离开罗浮仙舟，当然，这一趟的经历也会变成英雄故事中的后日谈吧。
t_en: Now that my mission is accomplished, the delegation will be leaving the Xianzhou Luofu soon. But of course, the experiences here will become an epilogue to the hero's stories.

t_ch: 这金色树苗也会成为仙舟日常风景的一部分，想必也是遂了英雄的心愿。
t_en: This golden sapling will become a part of the everyday scenery of the Xianzhou. I believe it's what he would have wanted.

t_ch: 一张式样普通的古筝。琴身与琴弦似乎由某种特别的金属铸造。
t_en: An unassuming ancient guzheng, its body and strings appear to be crafted from a unique kind of metal.

t_ch: 即便没有过客触碰琴弦，它依旧时不时迸出几个音符，像是在为某人哼唱不成调的曲子。
t_en: Even without a human's touch, it occasionally bursts out notes, as if humming an untuned melody for someone unseen.

t_ch: 请允许我真诚称赞你，这位女士，你的美丽如同纯真洁白的鸢尾花。
t_en: Please allow me to offer a sincere compliment, my fair lady. Your beauty is as pure as a snow-white iris.

t_ch: 这不是我的挚友吗？又见面了！
t_en: Well, if it isn't my dear friend! Glad to meet you again!

t_ch: 这个怪人又是谁啊？
t_en: Who is this weirdo?

t_ch: 啊，我是说这位骑士是谁……
t_en: Um, I mean, who is this knight...?

t_ch: 我名唤银枝，来自「纯美骑士团」，受邀成为卡勒瓦拉使团的一员，护送宝剑「米卡•奇瓦沙」回归仙舟。
t_en: I am Argenti, a member of the Knights of Beauty. I was invited as part of the Kalevala delegation to escort the legendary Miekka Kivessä back to the Xianzhou.

t_ch: 不知在下是否能有幸得知你的名字？
t_en: May I have the honor of knowing your name?

t_ch: ……我叫云璃。
t_en: ...My name is Yunli.

t_ch: 真是美丽的名字！不知道你是否听说过纯洁无瑕的伊德莉拉女神的圣名……
t_en: What a beautiful name! I wonder if you have ever heard the holy name of the pure and flawless Goddess Idrila...

t_ch: 啊，云璃，来了啊。你的消息倒是灵通…罢了，眼下这般场合，你可千万记得不可无礼。
t_en: Oh, you're here, Yunli. Didn't expect you to be so well-informed... Well, now that you're here, don't forget your manners.

t_ch: 无礼无礼…我才刚到这里有什么无礼的。真是的，天底下哪有像我这么懂礼貌的孙女啊？
t_en: Manners, manners... Come on, you won't find another granddaughter as polite and well-behaved as me!

t_ch: 罢了罢了，你能注意这是个庄重的场合就行。
t_en: Well, just remember this is a very serious ceremony.

t_ch: 帕沃尔先生，让您久等了，我们开始「赠剑仪式」吧。
t_en: Sorry for keeping you waiting, Mr. Paavo. Now, let the Sword-Gifting Ceremony begin.

t_ch: 云璃说自己没有偷剑，我愿意信她。
t_en: Yunli says she didn't steal the sword, and I am willing to believe her.

t_ch: 自始至终，闯进工造司的不速之客只有一名嫌疑人，那就是云璃小姐。
t_en: Throughout the entire incident, there was only one suspect who broke into the Artisanship Commission, and that was Miss Yunli.

t_ch: 云璃小姐想带走孤云，来到工造司大闹一番，却在途中被你阻止。
t_en: Miss Yunli caused a ruckus at the Artisanship Commission, trying to take away Guyun, but you stopped her in her tracks.

t_ch: 与你告别后，她假意离开，实则孤身闯进了武库，窃走了宝剑孤云。
t_en: After bidding farewell to you, Yunli pretended to leave but snuck into the arsenal alone and stole Guyun.

t_ch: 整个案件没有别的嫌疑人，我们也没有找到其他证据。
t_en: We haven't found any other suspects or evidence.

t_ch: 追问
t_en: (Continue questioning)

t_ch: 你的说法不太严谨，我、银枝还有使团成员也来过工造司。
t_en: Your statement isn't quite accurate. Argenti, the delegation members, and even me, we were all at the Artisanship Commission at some point.

t_ch: 你说的不错。但他们在送剑后便走了，负责交接的匠人证明他们不可能带剑离开。
t_en: True, but they all left after delivering the sword, and the craftsman overseeing the handover confirmed that they couldn't have left with the sword.

t_ch: 我们也调查过了，孤云交接前，工造司入口的机巧鸟拍到了{NICKNAME}离开…反倒是没有云璃姑娘离去的记录。
t_en: We looked into it as well. Before Guyun was handed over, the cycrane at the entrance of the Artisanship Commission captured {NICKNAME} leaving... but there's no record of Miss Yunli leaving.

t_ch: 砸坏了金人，我怎么还敢从正门出去嘛？
t_en: Why would I dare leave through the main entrance after smashing the aurumatons?

t_ch: 追问
t_en: (Continue questioning)

t_ch: （大毫说的好像没错啊……）
t_en: (What Dahao said seems right...)

t_ch: 没错，至少在那个时候，云璃并没有窃走宝剑，我们去的是金人仓库。
t_en: Exactly. So at least Yunli didn't steal the sword at that time, because we were at the aurumaton warehouse.

t_ch: 如果你的证言可信的话——没别的意思，毕竟每一份证据都应当被审视。
t_en: ...If your testimony is reliable. I'm not implying anything, but we need to scrutinize every piece of evidence.

t_ch: 追问
t_en: (Continue questioning)

t_ch: 我和云璃分别后，还特意发消息叮嘱了公输师傅。
t_en: After Yunli and I went our separate ways, I messaged Master Gongshu to inform him about the incident.

t_ch: 后来过了很久，公输师傅表示工造司已经验收了宝剑，至少到那刻为止，云璃是没有嫌疑的。
t_en: And much later, Master Gongshu told me the sword was confirmed safe. So up until that point, Yunli couldn't have stolen it.

t_ch: （我有什么证明方法吗？）
t_en: (Can I prove this?)

t_ch: 出示证人「公输师傅」
t_en: Present witness: Master Gongshu

t_ch: 你还给公输师傅还发了信息？我完全不知道！咳，这倒是个证据。
t_en: Wait, you messaged Master Gongshu? I had no idea! Well, that's some solid proof right there.

t_ch: 不过，也仅仅证明到那时为止，剑尚未被窃走。
t_en: However, it only proves the sword wasn't stolen up to that point.

t_ch: 我没有！
t_en: I didn't do it!

t_ch: 追问
t_en: (Continue questioning)

t_ch: 你好像漏掉了什么关键证据。
t_en: Looks like you're missing some crucial evidence.

t_ch: 整个武库并未有被人撬开的迹象，要说有什么证据，就只有剑匣了。
t_en: There are no signs of the arsenal being broken into. So, the only real evidence is the sword case.

t_ch: 没有被撬动痕迹本身就是可疑的证据。
t_en: No, the fact that there are no signs of a break-in is suspicious in itself.

t_ch: （该用哪个证物来说服大毫呢？）
t_en: (Which piece of evidence should I use to convince Dahao?)

t_ch: 出示证物「完好的锁扣」
t_en: Present evidence: intact lock

t_ch: 我仔细观察了剑匣的锁扣，它没有任何被暴力撬动、破坏的痕迹。
t_en: I carefully examined the lock of the sword case, and there were no signs of tampering or damage.

t_ch: 这能说明什么？
t_en: What does that mean?

t_ch: 剑匣打一开始就没锁上。
t_en: It means the case was never locked in the first place.

t_ch: 剑匣一开始有没有被锁上，这一点只有将宝剑送来工造司的人最清楚。
t_en: As to why the case was never locked in the first place, only the people who delivered it to the Artisanship Commission would know.

t_ch: 除此之外，我还在匣中找到一样东西。
t_en: Plus, I found something else inside the case.

t_ch: （该用哪个证物来进一步说服大毫呢？）
t_en: (Which piece of evidence should I use to further convince Dahao?)

t_ch: 出示证物「玫瑰花瓣」
t_en: Present evidence: rose petal

t_ch: 我在剑匣底部夹缝中，找到了这个。
t_en: I found this in the crevice at the bottom of the case.

t_ch: 玫瑰…花瓣？呃…多半是我搜查时大意了。这东西怎么会在剑匣里？
t_en: A... rose petal? Hmm... I must have missed it during the search. But why was there a petal inside the case?

t_ch: 我不清楚。但我知道，那位护送宝剑的纯美骑士，在他走过的地方总会留下一地难以打扫干净的玫瑰花瓣。
t_en: I don't know, but I do know that wherever that Knight of Beauty goes, he always leaves behind a trail of rose petals that's almost impossible to clean up.

t_ch: 出示
t_en: Present evidence

t_ch: 呃，也许…等等，让我再考虑一下。
t_en: On second thought... maybe let me think about it again.

t_ch: 你想清楚，我的逻辑可是无懈可击的。
t_en: Take your time. My reasoning is solid.

t_ch: …在我看来，这毫无疑问是一场「谋杀」。
t_en: ...In my estimation, there's no question about it. This is murder.

t_ch: 谋杀…这个词对我们太陌生了。在家族的保护下，匹诺康尼不可能出现任何伤亡。
t_en: Murder... Such a foreign word to us. The people of Penacony could never come to any harm while under the watchful protection of The Family.

t_ch: 在梦中，就算一个人被铁锤殴打千百下，充其量也只是在现实酒店中醒来。
t_en: In a dream, even if you're bashed a thousand times with a hammer, at worst you'll still wake up in reality in a hotel.

t_ch: 话不能这么说啊，就算结果构不成伤亡，这犯罪意图…也够明显了吧？你们特地过来封锁现场，不就是因为这案子性质恶劣？
t_en: Not necessarily. Even if the result does not constitute harm, the criminal intent is... clear enough? You specifically came here to cordon off the scene because of the unsavory nature of this case, right?

t_ch: 你说的也对。即便不会真的受伤，但好端端走在大马路上，就被人用锤子一顿乱砸…这绝不是该发生在匹诺康尼的事。
t_en: You're not wrong. Even if there are no casualties, being smashed over the head with a hammer in the middle of the street for no reason at all... this is not the kind of thing that happens on Penacony.

t_ch: 二位既然是橡木家系派来的，现场就任由你们调查了。我会在一旁待命。
t_en: You are guests of the Oak Family, so you may investigate the crime scene. I'll be on standby over here.

t_ch: 好姐姐，你究竟使的什么法子，让她转眼就相信咱们是什么「侦查人员」……
t_en: Are you a witch!? Just how did you manage to convince them in the blink of an eye that we're some kind of detectives...

t_ch: （如实相告）
t_en: (Tell the truth.)

t_ch: 只是一些「记忆」层面的暗示。如果一件事出现在所有人的记忆中…那么它就是「事实」。
t_en: Just some suggestions at the memory level. If something happens in everybody's memory... then that something becomes a fact.

t_ch: 若时间再充裕些，让她相信我是「同谐」本人垂迹也不是不可能。
t_en: If we had a little more time, I could have even convinced her I was Xipe the Harmony.

t_ch: （不做解释）
t_en: (Offer no explanation.)

t_ch: 只是一些「记忆」层面的暗示。如果一件事出现在所有人的记忆中…那么它就是「事实」。
t_en: Just some suggestions at the memory level. If something happens in everybody's memory... then that something becomes a fact.

t_ch: 比起说明——你要亲自体验一下么？
t_en: Rather than hearing endless explanations, would you like to experience it for yourself?

t_ch: 不用了不用了，您这本事还是留着对付敌人吧！
t_en: No no no, hey, you can save that for the enemy!

t_ch: 锵锵——是我赢咯。
t_en: Ding-a-ling — I won~

t_ch: …「花火大人的尸体」也能算是一个角色么？
t_en: ...Does "Miss Sparkle's body" even count as a real character?

t_ch: 怎么不能呀？那三个小剧场，可是每个都出现了「花火大人的尸体」哦。
t_en: Why not? "Miss Sparkle's body" appeared in all three of those skits just then.

t_ch: 虽说是有些刻意的强调，但也并无不可吧。那…这位桑博先生又是？
t_en: It's a little bit forced, but it's fine. So... who is this Mr. Sampo, then?

t_ch: 美丽的女士，我一早就向您坦白了——我真的什么都不知道，只是帮朋友送封信而已。
t_en: Beautiful lady, I already confessed everything to you earlier on — I really didn't know a thing. I just helped a friend send a letter, that's all.

t_ch: 原来，是我从最初就落入了愚者们的陷阱。
t_en: So I've been running a fool's errand this whole time.

t_ch: 亲爱的，何必这么认真呢？你不是玩得很开心吗——至少我很开心呀。
t_en: My dear, why so serious? Haven't you been having a great time? I have, at least.

t_ch: 你拿走「记忆」，我收获「欢愉」，我们能从彼此身上各取所需。既然这么投缘，你我又都是孤身一人…何不与我同行，一起跳支双人舞呢？
t_en: You take memories, and I get joy. We get what we need from each other. Seeing as we're such kindred spirits but also such loners... Why don't you come and work with me? We can dance ballet together~

t_ch: 哦…我是否可以理解为，一位假面愚者向我发出了合作的邀请？
t_en: Oh... am I understanding this correctly? A Masked Fool, inviting me to work together?

t_ch: 一场空前绝后的好戏就要在匹诺康尼上演了，来不及登台的人，就只能退居幕后。
t_en: A show of unprecedented splendor is about to be staged in Penacony. If you're too late to take the stage, you can work behind the scenes.

t_ch: 要欣赏这出戏剧，你只能凑上台前，站到聚光灯下，看一切围着你手舞足蹈——跟我走吧，这里才是最好的观众席！
t_en: If you want to appreciate this drama, you have to stand up under that spotlight as you take the stage and watch the dancing envelop you — come with me, this is the best seat in the house!

t_ch: …虽然不明白，你为何如此执着于破坏家族的藩篱。但我已说过，忆者帮不了你，另请高明吧。
t_en: ...I don't really understand why you're so fixated on destroying Family barriers, but as I said before, a Memokeeper can't help you. It's frankly above my pay grade.

t_ch: 另外，我也不觉得你孤单。哦，我不是指他，而是说你背后……
t_en: Also, I don't consider you a loner. Oh, I don't mean him, but rather behind you...

t_ch: 雇佣你来匹诺康尼的那位，应该不希望你和其他势力暗中来往吧？
t_en: The one who hired you to come to Penacony probably doesn't want you secretly linking up with other forces, right?

t_ch: …你怎么知道的？
t_en: ...How did you know?

t_ch: 虽说记忆就像深海，但想一鱼两吃…海里的鱼可未必合你胃口哦？
t_en: Memory is like the deep blue sea. But if you try to catch two fish with one cast... You might not be satisfied with your catch.

t_ch: 那你的意思，是要拒绝咯？
t_en: So you mean you're refusing?

t_ch: 忆者…你觉得你能安然离开？
t_en: Memokeeper... Did you think you could just leave?

t_ch: 为什么不能呢？
t_en: Why not?

t_ch: 亲爱的，不吓你了。你会知道答案的，迟早的事！今天就当浅浅认识下，什么时候改变心意了，就来「酒馆」找我吧——前提是要找得到，哈哈！
t_en: My dear, I don't want to scare you. You'll know the answer sooner or later! We're just getting to know each other a little today. If you ever change your mind, come find me at the tavern — that is, IF you can find me, haha!

t_ch: 我们走，桑…桑…博？
t_en: Let's go, Sam... Sampo?

t_ch: …桑博…是谁…？
t_en: ...Sampo... Who is... Sampo?

t_ch: 咦，我这是…这里不是贝洛伯格？
t_en: Eh, this is... This is Belobog, right?

t_ch: 我说了…为什么不能呢？
t_en: I said... why not?

t_ch: 别担心，太过粗暴的方式有违我的记忆美学，所以等我离开所有人的视线，你们就能重新想起一切了。以及……
t_en: Don't worry. Such a crude approach violates my aesthetics of memory, so once you leave my sight, you'll be able to remember everything again. And also...

t_ch: 对不起哦，亲爱的。心仪的舞伴…我已经有人选了。
t_en: I'm sorry, my dear. My dream dance partner... I've already got sights on someone else.

t_ch: …真是的，我又不会真的对她做什么。
t_en: ...Really, I wouldn't do anything to her.

t_ch: 别管那忆者了，来聊聊我的事吧。该帮的忙我都帮了，按照约定——
t_en: Don't worry about that Memokeeper. Let's talk about my thing. I've helped you out with everything you've asked, as agreed...

t_ch: 该把我的「面具」还给我了吧？
t_en: Shouldn't you give my mask back to me now?

t_ch: 从什么时候开始，假面愚者也要遵守约定了？
t_en: Since when did the Masked Fools suddenly start respecting agreements?

t_ch: ——哈哈，开玩笑的！我就爱看你这表情，拿去吧，酒馆地窖的钥匙，在左手边第三个桶里。别说是我给的。
t_en: ...Haha, just kidding! I love seeing that look on your face. Take this key to the tavern cellar. Third barrel on the left. You never saw me.

t_ch: 不过啊，桑博…时隔这么久，你怎么突然想起拿回面具了？这不像我认识的你呀……
t_en: But Sampo... After all this time, what made you suddenly want to take back your mask? This doesn't seem like the Sampo I know...

t_ch: 让我猜猜——雅利洛-VI大灾将至了，对么？
t_en: Let me guess: A catastrophe is coming to Jarilo-VI, am I right?

t_ch: 这个嘛，无可奉告。不过我很喜欢你说过的一句话——「好戏就要上演了，来不及登台的人，就只能退居幕后」——很有道理。
t_en: Well, no comment about that. But I loved that line you said, "If you're too late to take the stage, you can work behind the scenes" — That really makes sense.

t_ch: 偶尔，我老桑博也想上台献丑一番。
t_en: Sometimes, even an old-timer like me feels like taking the stage and making a fool out of myself.

t_ch: 要是你万策尽了，记得联系我哦。我这个人很重视情谊。
t_en: If you're all out of options, please get in touch with me. I'm the kind of person who values friendship highly.

t_ch: 免了吧，你离贝洛伯格远一点，就是帮了我大忙啦。毕竟花火小姐寻求的「欢愉」…桑博可不敢恭维啊。
t_en: Forget it. You'll be doing me a favor if you stay away from Belobog. After all, the Elation that Miss Sparkle seeks... isn't something ol' Sampo can stand by.

t_ch: 走了，替我向乔瓦尼问个好。
t_en: Go. Tell Giovanni I said hi.

t_ch: 知道啦，我就是随口说说，别当真嘛。一个两个都这样……
t_en: Fine, I'm just spitballing here. Don't take it so seriously...

t_ch: ——何必那么认真呢？
t_en: ...Why so serious?

t_ch: 那么接下来…我们来聊聊你这位「同伴」吧。
t_en: Then on to the next order of business — let's discuss your companion, shall we?

t_ch: 「忆庭的忆者，我注意你很久了。」
t_en: "Memokeeper of the Garden of Recollection: I've been watching you for quite some time."

t_ch: 「入住酒店时，你就在偷看我。来到梦境后，我去哪，你就跟到哪：商业街的橱窗里，艾迪恩公园的水面下，甚至酒杯的倒影里，无处不在！」
t_en: "You stole a glance at me when you first entered the hotel. You followed me wherever I went inside the dreamscape: In the windows along the commercial street, in the pondwater reflections of the Aideen Park, even in the reflections of the wine glass... Everywhere!"

t_ch: 「——亲爱的，你是不是暗恋我呀？好呀，既然你对我这么感兴趣，咱们就玩个游戏吧。」
t_en: "...My dear, do you have a crush on me? Well then, seeing as you're so interested in me, let's play a little game."

t_ch: 「我在奥帝购物中心附近给你留了道谜题，解开它，证明你有能力取悦我。如果你能做到…我们也不是不能谈谈。」
t_en: "I left a puzzle for you near Oti Mall. Solve it and prove that you're capable of pleasing me. If you can solve it... then we can talk."

t_ch: 「别让我等太久，亲爱的。在匹诺康尼被搅得天翻地覆前，试着找到我~抓住我~阻止我吧~」
t_en: "Don't leave me waiting, my dear. Before Penacony gets flipped upside down, try to find me~ catch me~ and stop me~"

t_ch: 事实上，来到匹诺康尼的忆者不止一人，她把我和其他人弄混了。不过也无妨……
t_en: There's actually more than one Memokeeper who's come to Penacony — she mixed me up with someone else. But no harm, no foul...

t_ch: 面具中提到的地址就是这里，只是没想到…花火小姐口中的游戏竟是在匹诺康尼街头「大开杀戒」。
t_en: The address that the mask gave you is right here. But you never would have thought... that when Miss Sparkle mentioned a game, she was talking about wanton slaughter on the streets of Penacony.

t_ch: 收到如此直接的挑衅，我也多少被激起了些好胜心呢。
t_en: Such a direct act of provocation is enough to get my competitive side worked up too.

t_ch: 姐，您饶了我吧，她说的这些我真不知道——都给你查过脑子了。
t_en: Madam, forgive me. I had no idea about anything she said — As you saw, I went through a brain scan and everything.

t_ch: 我只是朋友一场，帮她送个信而已——谁知这不是送信，是下战书来了啊！都说两军交战不斩来使，您放我走吧，你们玩得太大了，我实在陪不起……
t_en: I am just a friend, helping her deliver a letter, that's all — I never knew this was actually a declaration of warfare! They say you shouldn't kill the messenger — so maybe you can just, you know, let me go. You all are the big shots here. I'm really not on your level...

t_ch: 很害怕的表情呢。放心，既然此事与你无关，我不会为难你的。就当陪我走走吧。
t_en: What a frightened look you have. Don't worry. Since this has nothing to do with you, I won't be suspecting you of anything. Why don't you come take a stroll with me?

t_ch: 哎哟，看来这「侦探助手」我是逃不掉了…行吧行吧，唉，都什么事儿啊！
t_en: Ah, seems like I can't escape being an assistant detective... Fine. It's all fine, no big deal!

t_ch: 二位想知道什么尽管开口。
t_en: If there's anything you'd like to know, please ask away.

t_ch: 关于受害者…
t_en: Regarding the victim...

t_ch: 受害者的身份是？
t_en: Who was the victim?

t_ch: 是位星际和平公司的职员，叫沙马里。听目击者说，有个人高马大的壮汉，穿得宽袍大袖，和沙马里擦肩而过时，突然一锤子砸了下去…
t_en: An IPC employee named Shamari. Eyewitnesses reported that a tall, strong man wearing a large robe with long sleeves walked right past Shamari, then suddenly assaulted him with a hammer...

t_ch: 沙马里当场就不行了，一下从梦里惊醒了过来。那凶手也转眼消失不见。
t_en: Shamari collapsed on the spot and woke up from the dream. The murderer vanished in a flash.

t_ch: 唉，一听就是花火干的。她最擅长的就是变化成其他人的样子。
t_en: That sounds just like Sparkle. Her best asset is transforming her appearance to look like someone else.

t_ch: 关于现场的美梦剧团…
t_en: Regarding the Sweet Dreams Troupe present at the scene of the crime...

t_ch: 那两个小家伙是…？
t_en: Who are those two little guys?

t_ch: 似乎是袭击者从别处偷来的美梦剧团，故意放在这儿的…真气人，简直就是不把公共秩序放在眼里。
t_en: Seems like the assailant stole the troupe from elsewhere and deliberately placed them here. What nerve... They don't even have the slightest consideration for public order.

t_ch: 那俩东西一直不肯说话，不知道二位能否撬开它们的嘴。
t_en: Those two have clammed up. I wonder if you two could help pry open their mouths.

t_ch: 看来那就是花火留给你的谜题了。
t_en: Looks like a puzzle that Sparkle has left behind for you.

t_ch: 关于现场的证物…
t_en: Regarding evidence at the scene of the crime...

t_ch: 有发现什么线索吗？
t_en: Any leads yet?

t_ch: 因为是突发事件，没什么有用的线索，只有袭击者使用的凶器，和受害者的证件……
t_en: This is a developing case, so there aren't many clues yet. All we have to go by are the weapon used by the assailant and the victim's ID...

t_ch: 那人没带走凶器，大概是在向我们挑衅吧…太可恨了。
t_en: They didn't even take the weapon with them. Sounds like they're trying to provoke us... Despicable.

t_ch: 能传唤受害者吗？
t_en: Can we summon the victim?

t_ch: 那位沙马里先生现在在哪里？我想和他见一面。
t_en: Where is Mr. Shamari right now? I'd like to meet him.

t_ch: 恐怕不行。橡木家系出面安抚了他的情绪，沙马里先生对梦中的不可思议表示理解，前往其他梦境继续度假了。
t_en: I'm afraid you can't. The Oaks came forward and calmed him down. Mr. Shamari expressed that he understands the unpredictable nature of dreams, and went on to vacation in another dreamscape.

t_ch: 还是不要打扰他了。客人们的体验永远排在第一优先。
t_en: Best not to bother him then. Guests' experiences always have to come first.

t_ch: 等找到他，怕不是半个匹诺康尼都要被花火送回现实了……
t_en: I'm afraid by the time we find him, half of Penacony will have been sent back to reality by Sparkle...

t_ch: 看来我们不得不陪花火小姐玩这场游戏了呢。
t_en: Looks like we'll have to play this little game of Sparkle's then.

t_ch: 喂！亲爱的忆者？你要去哪儿？不会想逃吧？不会是怕了吧？流光忆庭就这？就这？就这？
t_en: Hey! My dear Memokeeper? Where do you think you're going! Don't think you can escape, hmm? Are you afraid? Is that it for the Garden of Recollection? Is that it? Is that all?

t_ch: 哎！这都是花火的个人发言，可不代表假面愚者啊！
t_en: Hey! That's Sparkle who's saying all that! She doesn't represent the Masked Fools!

t_ch: 喂！亲爱的忆者？你要去哪儿？不会是想逃吧？不会是怕了吧？流光忆庭就这？就这？就这？
t_en: Hey! My dear Memokeeper? Where do you think you're going! Don't think you can escape, hmm? Are you afraid? Is that it for the Garden of Recollection? Is that it? Is that all?

t_ch: 黑天鹅大人，我们总算等到您了！我是此地的捕头，旁边那位则是协助我一起办案的捕快。
t_en: Lady Black Swan, you're here! I'm the constable, and this here assisting me is the bailiff for this case.

t_ch: 哟，寒腿叔叔，你怎么叛变了？
t_en: Mr. Cold Feet — Why did you do it?

t_ch: 还真开口说话了！黑天鹅小姐，它们果真是冲着你来的——
t_en: So they can talk after all! Miss Black Swan, they're heading straight for you...

t_ch: 少插嘴，有你说话的份吗？咳咳…听闻黑天鹅大人智计超凡，想来定能解开这起「花火大人遇害案」的真相！
t_en: Don't interrupt. I wasn't talking to you. Ahem... I understand Lady Black Swan has quite the intellect. I'm sure you'll be able to get to the bottom of the Sparkle murder case!

t_ch: 「花火大人遇害案」？受害者不是星际和平公司的沙马里吗？
t_en: The Sparkle Murder case? Wasn't the victim Shamari from the IPC?

t_ch: 您弄错了，黑天鹅大人。遇害的分明是金槌家的家主，金槌花火大人啊！
t_en: You're wrong, Lady Black Swan. The victim is evidently none other than the matriarch of the Goldhammer Family: Miss Sparkle Goldhammer!

t_ch: …哦，看来这是花火小姐自导自演的小剧场，和实际案件并无关联。「金槌」是她的姓氏吗？
t_en: ...Oh, I guess Miss Sparkle has written herself into the skit here, and this has nothing to do with the actual case. Goldhammer — is that her real surname?

t_ch: 没听说过啊，随口编的吧。我看「花火」也不像什么真名。
t_en: Never heard of it. Sounds made up. Sparkle doesn't sound like a real name, either.

t_ch: 今天本应是花火大人在金槌府邸接见手下三位勇士的日子，可当勇士们走进府邸后，看见的却是「花火大人的尸体」。
t_en: Miss Sparkle was supposed to receive three valiant warriors at the Goldhammer residence today but upon their arrival, all they found was her body.

t_ch: 花火大人的脑袋被锤子砸开了花，当场毙命…多么残忍的暴行啊！
t_en: Miss Sparkle had been smashed over the head by a hammer, and died on the spot... Such a brutal act of violence!

t_ch: 据我调查，案发前日，三位勇士本应押送一批货物回到领地内，谁料在路上遭遇了山贼泯灭帮的袭击。
t_en: My investigations discovered that the three warriors were supposed to escort supplies back to their territory the day before, but they were unexpectedly ambushed by the Annihilation Gang.

t_ch: 三人九死一生，好不容易捡回一条命，但货物却被劫得不剩多少了。所以他们每个人都有为了逃避责任而杀害花火大人的动机！
t_en: They narrowly escaped and barely got away with their lives, finding their supplies almost completely raided. So they all shared a motive for killing Miss Sparkle — to escape punishment!

t_ch: 如此看来，是三位勇士联手行凶？
t_en: So the three of them teamed up to kill her?

t_ch: …这是结案了？
t_en: ...Is the case closed?

t_ch: 不对，凶手只能有一个人！三个人都是凶手还算什么疑案…稍等…稍等……
t_en: No, there can only be one murderer! It's not an unsolved mystery if there are three murderers... Hang on... Wait...

t_ch: 实在圆不上就算了吧……
t_en: If you can't justify it, don't bother...

t_ch: 有了！他们三人为争夺二把手的位置，关系极其紧张，根本不可能合作！所以必然是其中一人单独作案！
t_en: Got it! Those three were all vying against each other to be number two. They couldn't have cooperated on anything! So it must have been one of them acting alone.

t_ch: 其实不太讲得通，就算这三个人关系不好，可是……
t_en: That actually doesn't make much sense. Even if they were on bad terms, those three...

t_ch: 请黑天鹅大人开始调查吧！案发现场留下了许多线索，一定能帮助您锁定真凶。
t_en: Please begin your investigation, Lady Black Swan! There are lots of clues at the crime scene that are sure to help you apprehend the real culprit.

t_ch: 我已备好案件卷宗，您可以向我了解更多有关嫌疑人的信息。
t_en: I've got the case file right here. If you want to learn more about the suspects, talk to me.

t_ch: 黑天鹅大人，请容我向您介绍本案的三名嫌疑人——佐罗、钟珊和阿佑。
t_en: Lady Black Swan, please allow me to introduce the three suspects to you — Lefton, Zhongshan, and Wright.

t_ch: 佐罗是花火大人部下悍将，在对抗山贼时，奋勇抗敌，<color=#dbc291ff>右手不幸被斩断了</color>。现在正在练习用左手写字和拿筷子，真是可怜啊。
t_en: Lefton is one of Miss Sparkle's chief lieutenants, whose <color=#dbc291ff>right hand was unfortunately severed</color> while he was valiantly fighting off the Annihilation Gang. He's now learning how to write and hold a fork with his left hand — how tough that must be!

t_ch: 钟珊这人胆子比较小，遇见山贼转身就跑，结果撞到了山岩上，<color=#dbc291ff>整张脸都破相了</color>。可惜可惜，以前他可是城中难得一见的美男子啊。
t_en: Then there's Zhongshan — this guy's a coward who ran away as soon as the group spotted some bandits along the road. He ended up smashing into the side of a cliff and <color=#dbc291ff>getting his face disfigured</color>. It's a real shame, as he used to be one of the rare handsome men left around town.

t_ch: 最后是阿佑，这家伙贪得无厌，好不容易逃出生天，竟打算私吞剩下的货物，谎称是被山贼全部抢走了…佐罗和钟珊气不过，就<color=#dbc291ff>把他的腿打断了</color>。
t_en: Last of all comes Wright. This guy was so insatiably greedy that he still came back to try and embezzle the rest of the supplies after they narrowly escaped with their lives, lying about how the gang had taken off with everything... Lefton and Zhongshan were so incensed that they <color=#dbc291ff>broke his legs</color>.

t_ch: 等等，我给绕晕了…这左中右都是啥来着？
t_en: Hang on. I'm confused here... Lefton... Wright? Is this some kind of joke?

t_ch: 怎么样，您记住了吗？需不需要我再讲一遍？
t_en: What? Where did you get that idea from? Do you need me to go over it all over again?

t_ch: 再讲一遍。
t_en: Yes please.

t_ch: 我倒是都记住了。不过…看来「侦探助手」先生还有疑虑啊…那，麻烦你再复述一遍？
t_en: Got it. But... it sounds like Mr. Assistant Detective has his own misgivings... Would you like to go over it again?

t_ch: 佐罗是花火大人部下悍将，在对抗山贼时，奋勇抗敌，<color=#dbc291ff>右手不幸被斩断了</color>。现在正在练习用左手写字和拿筷子，真是可怜啊。
t_en: Lefton is one of Miss Sparkle's chief lieutenants, whose <color=#dbc291ff>right hand was unfortunately severed</color> while he was valiantly fighting off the Annihilation Gang. He's now learning how to write and hold a fork with his left hand — how tough that must be!

t_ch: 钟珊这人胆子比较小，遇见山贼转身就跑，结果撞到了山岩上，<color=#dbc291ff>整张脸都破相了</color>。可惜可惜，以前他可是城中难得一见的美男子啊。
t_en: Then there's Zhongshan — this guy's a coward who ran away as soon as the group spotted some bandits along the road. He ended up smashing into the side of a cliff and <color=#dbc291ff>getting his face disfigured</color>. It's a real shame, as he used to be one of the rare handsome men left around town.

t_ch: 最后是阿佑，这家伙贪得无厌，好不容易逃出生天，竟打算私吞剩下的货物，谎称是被山贼全部抢走了…佐罗和钟珊气不过，就<color=#dbc291ff>把他的腿打断了</color>。
t_en: Last of all comes Wright. This guy was so insatiably greedy that he still came back to try and embezzle the rest of the supplies after they narrowly escaped with their lives, lying about how the gang had taken off with everything... Lefton and Zhongshan were so incensed that they <color=#dbc291ff>broke his legs</color>.

t_ch: 以上就是三位嫌疑人全部的信息了。
t_en: That's all the intel we have on the three suspects.

t_ch: 不必了。
t_en: That won't be necessary.

t_ch: 不必了，我都记住了。
t_en: That won't be necessary. I've memorized every detail.

t_ch: 真不愧是忆者。
t_en: No wonder you're a Memokeeper.

t_ch: 就这点「证物」？这叫人怎么答题嘛……
t_en: Is that all the evidence? How are we meant to solve this with so little to go on...

t_ch: 咳咳！
t_en: *coughs*

t_ch: 吓我一跳！你什么时候跑出来的？
t_en: You scared me! When did you pop out?

t_ch: 你们发现的两件证物，是和沙马里遇袭相关的东西。花火大人物尽其用，将它们也当做了谜题的线索。
t_en: The two pieces of evidence that you found are related to the attack on Shamari. Miss Sparkle didn't leave them lying around for no reason — they are clues to the puzzle.

t_ch: 但还有一些别的线索，被放在了更不碍事的地方……
t_en: But, there are still some other clues that were placed in other areas so they won't spoil the crime scene...

t_ch: 这是否有些多此一举了？
t_en: Isn't that a bit unnecessary?

t_ch: 不不，如果在犯罪现场放一堆无关紧要的东西，不就成扰乱搜查了嘛！花火大人可是守法公民，绝不给家族添麻烦！
t_en: No no, it's disruptive to have a stack of irrelevant things crowding a crime scene! Miss Sparkle is a law-abiding citizen who'd never bring extra trouble to The Family!

t_ch: 她人还怪好的哦……
t_en: Seems quite nice of her, if you ask me...

t_ch: 看来花火小姐是想为调查再多添几分乐趣，我们就收下她的好意吧。请来这边，「侦探助手」先生。
t_en: Looks like Miss Sparkle is adding a little spice to the investigation. Let's take her up on her offer then — Mr. Assistant Detective, this way.

t_ch: 这张告示…似乎是花火留给我们的提示。
t_en: This notice... seems like a hint for us from Miss Sparkle.

t_ch: 刚听着还像个古代背景的故事，怎么突然冒出个人脸识别系统？
t_en: That sounded like a story from ancient times... and why did the facial recognition system suddenly go out?

t_ch: 很有她的风格，不是么？
t_en: This is so her style, isn't it?

t_ch: 嗯…这个小巧的装置是做什么用的，可以为我解释一下吗？看上去像是个按钮。
t_en: Mmm... What's this little device here for? Could you please let me know? It looks like a little button.

t_ch: 别按！黑天鹅大人，千万别按！
t_en: Don't push it! Lady Black Swan, whatever you do — don't push it!

t_ch: 这是花火大人留下的「相互保证毁灭」按钮，一旦按下，花火大人会立刻和整个匹诺康尼一起灰飞烟灭！
t_en: That's the "Mutually Assured Destruction" button that Miss Sparkle installed. Once pushed, Miss Sparkle and the entirety of Penacony will instantaneously go up in smoke!

t_ch: 与此同时，花火大人也持有一个相同的按钮。一旦她按下，您就会立刻和整个匹诺康尼一起灰飞烟灭！
t_en: Miss Sparkle also has her own button. The second she pushes hit, you and the entirety of Penacony will instantaneously go up in smoke!

t_ch: 这听起来…似乎没什么区别？
t_en: That sounds like... both buttons can do the exact same thing?

t_ch: 的确没什么区别。
t_en: Correct — exactly the same.
Oh...

t_ch: 您不问问这是为什么吗？
t_en: Aren't you going to ask me why?

t_ch: 那…这是为什么呢？
t_en: Okay... Why?

t_ch: 因为她公平。
t_en: Because she is fair.

t_ch: 有毛病啊！
t_en: This is nuts!

t_ch: 这又是什么东西…毛绒玩具？
t_en: What's this... a plush toy?

t_ch: 且不论形象为何是星穹列车的列车长，这个玩偶也和案件有关吗？
t_en: Regardless of why it's designed like the conductor aboard the Astral Express, this doll — Is it connected to the case?

t_ch: 您有所不知，这在推理故事中叫「红鲱鱼」，意思是「误导思路的假线索」！
t_en: Have you ever heard of something called a Red Herring in detective stories? It's a fake clue that leads you down the wrong path!

t_ch: 原来如此。但你这么直截了当地说出来，它不就没有用了吗？
t_en: I see. But when it's so obvious, doesn't that defeat the purpose?

t_ch: 这、这不是怕二位没能察觉花火小姐的巧思，误以为她随便扔了个没用的线索……
t_en: Th—That's just in case you didn't realize the ingenuity of Miss Sparkle, and were worried about her throwing out a random useless clue...

t_ch: 多谢你的良苦用心，那我们就假装被「红鲱鱼」误导了吧。嗯…这个玩偶也和案件有关吗？
t_en: I see — thank you for your keen insight. Then let's pretend we were misled by a Red Herring. Hmm... this doll... Could it be connected to the case?

t_ch: 也不必这么给她面子吧……
t_en: You don't have to play along that hard, you know...

t_ch: 看来没有别的线索了，我们回案发现场吧。
t_en: Doesn't look like there are any more clues. Let's head back to the crime scene.

t_ch: 接下来，就该锁定凶手的身份了吧？可这些证物…我看了半天，也没觉得哪个可以指认真凶啊。
t_en: Next, should we pin down the identity of the perpetrator? This evidence... I've been staring at it all day but, there's nothing that can identify the perp.

t_ch: 不如将思路反转过来，思考「谁不可能是凶手」呢？毕竟嫌疑人只有三位，一一排除也不失为一种方法。
t_en: Why don't we flip the question and start by asking who COULDN'T be the murderer? After all, there are only three suspects, so elimination could be a valid method.

t_ch: 听这话，你是已经有头绪了？快说来听听。
t_en: If you put it like that, then do you already have someone in mind? Tell me, c'mon.

t_ch: 请黑天鹅大人开始调查吧！案发现场留下了许多线索，一定能帮助您锁定真凶。
t_en: Please begin your investigation, Lady Black Swan! There are lots of clues at the crime scene that are sure to help you apprehend the real culprit.

t_ch: 真凶是「阿佑」。
t_en: The murderer is Wright.

t_ch: 嘟嘟嘟嘟嘟——！回答正确，真不愧是黑天鹅大人！按照游戏规则，我将告诉您下一道谜题的地点。
t_en: Beep beep beep — ! You have answered correctly. As expected, you didn't disappoint, Lady Black Swan. Game rules stipulate I must tell you where the next puzzle is.

t_ch: 啊？下一个谜题，这跟说好的不一样啊。
t_en: Huh? The next puzzle? I didn't sign up for this.

t_ch: 看来，胜负的关键不在谜题本身，如果我们不能看穿花火小姐古怪行径下的真实意图…恐怕这场追赶永远不会结束。
t_en: Looks like the key to winning lies not in the puzzle itself, but in the motives behind Miss Sparkle's strange behavior... I'm afraid this chase may just go on forever.

t_ch: 我得提醒你一句，那花火可是个假面愚者，你打算在一个假面愚者的脑袋里找逻辑？
t_en: I have to remind you that Sparkle is a Masked Fool. Do you plan on finding logic in the mind of a Masked Fool?

t_ch: 我的确是这么考虑的。即便主观不存在规律，行为也一定会留下某种共性——记忆是不会说谎的，它或许比那女孩本人更了解她的本质。
t_en: That's exactly what I plan on doing. Even if it's just subjective, there must be an overall principle behind the behavior — memories cannot lie, and hers may just understand more about her than she does herself.

t_ch: 眼下，我们就先顺从她的心思，前往下一题的所在吧。希望这次能赶在猎犬家系之前，如果可以，我并不想使用忆者的能力。
t_en: Right now, let's follow her train of thought and head to the next puzzle. I hope this time we can get ahead of the Bloodhound Family. I want to try and avoid using my Memokeeper powers.

t_ch: 哦，黑天鹅警探，桑博警探，你们可终于来了！
t_en: Oh, Detective Black Swan and Detective Sampo. You finally made it!

t_ch: 要是没有你们的帮助，我们可真不知道该怎么办才好！
t_en: We don't know what we'd do without your help!

t_ch: 呃，你们为什么要这样说话？是什么我老桑博不懂的艺术形式吗……
t_en: Uh, why are you talking like that? Is this some sort of artistic performance that young people are into these days?

t_ch: 说话？我们说话的方式有什么问题吗？副警长阁下，我说话的方式很奇怪吗？
t_en: Is there something wrong with the way we are talking? Deputy Sheriff, am I talking in a strange way?

t_ch: 当然不，警长阁下，您说话的方式比我外婆家后院苹果树上趴着的猫还要稀松平常。
t_en: Of course not, Sheriff. The way you talk is no stranger than the cat that climbs the apple tree in my grandma's backyard.

t_ch: 这次的小剧场是现代风格呢。
t_en: The skit this time is a modernist one.

t_ch: 还是谈谈这起难解的疑案吧！这桩案件的受害者，是一位名叫「花火」的银河富商……
t_en: Let's discuss this difficult case, then! The victim is a galactic business magnate named Sparkle...

t_ch: 又是花火？她就这么热衷于把自己写死？
t_en: Her again? Sparkle really is obsessed with scripting her own death.

t_ch: 哦，乐子神在上，你要是再拆我的台，我发誓一定会狠狠地踢你的屁股！
t_en: Oh, for The Laughter — if you undercut me one more time, I swear I'm gonna kick you in the butt!

t_ch: 咳咳…花火大人来到这家服装店，想为自己购买一条崭新的领带。可她进入试衣间后，久久都没有出来。
t_en: Ahem... Miss Sparkle came to this fashion store to buy herself a brand-new tie... She didn't come back out.

t_ch: 店员进入试衣间查看情况，却发现了「花火大人的尸体」！她在试衣时被人勒死了！
t_en: After a long time, the shopkeeper went in to see what was going on, but instead discovered Miss Sparkle's body! She had been strangled to death!

t_ch: 而现场共有三位嫌疑人，分别是皮皮西店员阿佑、狐人赌徒钟珊、和智械富商佐罗。
t_en: There were three suspects on the scene, namely a Pepeshi shopkeeper named Wright, a foxian gambler named Zhongshan, and a wealthy Intellitron trader called Lefton.

t_ch: 这三位也是老朋友呢。
t_en: Our old friends.

t_ch: 还不是为了方便你们记忆！这次还给他们补上了种族细节，方便区分。
t_en: It's not just to aid your memory! We've added more descriptions to help tell them apart.

t_ch: 花火大人真是宅心仁厚！
t_en: Miss Sparkle is so gracious!

t_ch: 最后，根据监控录像，目击者证词，还有各种乱七八糟的证据，凶手一定在这三个人里——而且他们绝对不是共犯。
t_en: Based on surveillance footage, witness testimony, and various pieces of evidence, the killer is ultimately among these three — and they were not working in cahoots.

t_ch: 你彻底懒得圆了是吧？
t_en: You're too lazy to be a suspect, right?

t_ch: 哦，乐子神在上，多和你说一句话，都玷污了我高贵圣洁的魂灵！
t_en: Oh, for The Laughter — if I talk any more with you, it'll make my pure soul filthy!

t_ch: 黑天鹅警探，档案室在这边。您可以向我了解更多有关嫌疑人的信息。相信您一定可以拨开迷雾，找到杀害花火大人的凶手。
t_en: Detective Black Swan, the dossiers are over here. You can learn more about the suspects from me. I trust you will be able to cut through the hogwash and find out who Miss Sparkle's killer is.

t_ch: 我来为您介绍下，本案的几名嫌疑人，身份都不复杂——
t_en: Let me introduce the identities of these suspects to you. It's very straightforward —

t_ch: 首先是店员阿佑，他是个皮皮西人，身高还不到花火大人的腰，这一点常被人取笑，令他变得有些阴暗。
t_en: First of all is the shopkeeper, Wright. He's one of the Pepeshi people and only stands as tall as Sparkle's waist, which is often a source of teasing for him. He can get pretty salty about it.

t_ch: 然后是富商佐罗。他想加入匹诺康尼的商事集团，同花火大人是竞争关系。也许这就是他的杀人动机。
t_en: Next up, you have the merchant Lefton. He wants to join up with the Penacony trading guild, and is in direct competition with Miss Sparkle. That could be a motive for killing her.

t_ch: 最后是赌徒钟珊。他曾在一场赌局中输给花火大人，不得不交出自己的传家之宝，可能正因如此，他对花火大人怀恨在心。
t_en: Finally there is the gambler, Zhongshan. He lost a bet to Miss Sparkle, and he had to hand over his family heirloom. It's possible he harbored a grudge against her.

t_ch: 这就是全部的信息了。需要我再复述一遍吗？
t_en: That's all the information we have. Do you need me to repeat it?

t_ch: 想再听听嫌疑人信息。
t_en: I'd like to hear about the suspects one more time.

t_ch: （抱歉打断您的梦境体验，{NICKNAME}{M#先生}{F#女士}。这枚梦泡中似乎没有这样的片段。）
t_en: (Sorry to interrupt your dreamscape experience, {M#Mr.}{F#Madam} {NICKNAME}. That segment doesn't seem to be in this dream bubble.)

t_ch: （但请放心，我来为您重播一遍方才的记录。）
t_en: (But don't worry. I'll replay the records for you.)

t_ch: 首先是店员阿佑，他是个皮皮西人，身高还不到花火大人的腰，这一点常被人取笑，令他变得有些阴暗。
t_en: First of all is the shopkeeper, Wright. He's one of the Pepeshi people and only stands as tall as Sparkle's waist, which is often a source of teasing for him. He can get pretty salty about it.

t_ch: 然后是富商佐罗。他想加入匹诺康尼的商事集团，同花火大人是竞争关系。也许这就是他的杀人动机。
t_en: Next up, you have the merchant Lefton. He wants to join up with the Penacony trading guild, and is in direct competition with Miss Sparkle. That could be a motive for killing her.

t_ch: 最后是赌徒钟珊。他曾在一场赌局中输给花火大人，不得不交出自己的传家之宝，可能正因如此，他对花火大人怀恨在心。
t_en: Finally there is the gambler, Zhongshan. He lost a bet to Miss Sparkle, and he had to hand over his family heirloom. It's possible he harbored a grudge against her.

t_ch: 不必了。
t_en: That won't be necessary.

t_ch: 不必了，我记得住。
t_en: That won't be necessary. I remember it all.

t_ch: 看来和第一案一样，现场只有这两件证物。剩下的…又给她藏里面了吧？
t_en: So it's just like the first case, then — only two pieces of evidence. The rest... hidden inside her memories again, right?

t_ch: 花火大人不想扰乱侦查嘛！
t_en: Miss Sparkle doesn't want to distract your investigation!

t_ch: 好一个「不想扰乱侦查」，这花火真是个遵纪守法的好公民——那她怎么不去自首呢？
t_en: She doesn't want to "distract" our investigation? What a law-abiding citizen this Sparkle is — why doesn't she surrender, then?

t_ch: 一个小板凳，倒还算得上正常。上面画了两个小脚印——还是用蜡笔画的。
t_en: A little stool that seems quite normal. There are two small footprints drawn on it in crayon.

t_ch: 比常人小一半的脚印，应该是皮皮西人留下的吧。
t_en: The footprints, which are half as big as standard, must belong to a Pepeshi person.

t_ch: 也可能只是这人的画技非同凡响。
t_en: Perhaps it's just that this person's drawing skills are extraordinary.

t_ch: 原来里面存了个戒指，这也是证物吧。为什么要多此一举放在机关里呢。
t_en: So there's a ring inside. This is evidence, too. Why was it planted inside another device?

t_ch: 因为这戒指是在受害者口中发现的，要还原现场啊。
t_en: Because this ring was found inside the victim's mouth. We want to reconstruct the scene of the crime.

t_ch: 在受害者口中…？
t_en: In the victim's MOUTH...?

t_ch: 哎，这戒指侧面还刻了一行字：「钟珊家传」……
t_en: Hey, there's something engraved inside the ring: "Zhongshan Family Heirloom..."

t_ch: …什么味道啊这是——地上怎么有条烟熏鱼？！
t_en: ...What's that smell? Why is there a smoked red herring there?

t_ch: 桑博先生，仔细看看…这是一条上好的「烟熏红鲱鱼」。
t_en: Mr. Sampo, take a closer look... this is a wonderful smoked red herring.

t_ch: 救命啊，这笑话真的太冷了……
t_en: Somebody save me from this cringe joke...

t_ch: 即便在我认识的愚者中，花火小姐的幽默感也别具一格呢。
t_en: Even among the fools I know, Miss Sparkle's sense of humor stands out from the rest.

t_ch: 你见过很多假面愚者吗？
t_en: Have you dealt with many Masked Fools before?

t_ch: 不多，也就四五位吧，毕竟我更喜欢沐浴在阳光下的，令人温暖幸福的「记忆」，它们通常属于更善良的人。
t_en: No, only a handful. After all, I prefer warm memories baked in sunshine. They're the ones that usually belong to kind people.

t_ch: 应该没别的了，咱们出去吧。
t_en: That should be it. Let's head out.

t_ch: 我得向你表示感谢，「侦探助手」先生。不过…这次的谜题似乎有些奇怪。
t_en: I have to thank you, Mr. Assistant Detective. But... the puzzle this time is pretty strange.

t_ch: 你已经看出凶手是谁了？
t_en: Have you found out who the killer is?

t_ch: 只是一些浅浅的直觉…总之，我们先去向那位警长汇报吧。
t_en: Just some shallow intuition... Anyway, let's report back to the sheriff for now.

t_ch: 哦！乐子神在上！没想到黑天鹅警探这么快就回来了！怎么样？你找到凶手了吗？
t_en: Oh, for The Laughter! Didn't think I'd be seeing Detective Black Swan so soon! What's that? You've found out who the killer is?

t_ch: 姑且算是吧。
t_en: Pretty much.

t_ch: 真不愧是黑天鹅警探，你就像我弗兰克姨夫家车库顶棚上的那窝喜鹊一样机敏！
t_en: You live up to your reputation, Detective Black Swan. As smart as the nest of magpies roosting in the roof of my uncle Frank's garage!

t_ch: 好家伙，直接给您换了种鸟类。
t_en: This guy is mixing up his birds here.

t_ch: 那么请问——凶手是谁，又有什么证据能证明他是凶手呢？
t_en: Then, go ahead — who is the killer, and what evidence do you have to prove the case?

t_ch: 指认凶手。
t_en: Identify the murderer.

t_ch: 好啊！那就让我们鉴赏一下您的推理吧。
t_en: Good! Then let us appreciate your powers of deduction.

t_ch: 不指认凶手。
t_en: Do not identify the murderer.

t_ch: 在我看来，这起案件应该不存在凶手。
t_en: In my opinion, there is no murderer for this case.

t_ch: …您这是什么意思？
t_en: ...What do you mean by that?

t_ch: 证据就是这条领带，对么？
t_en: This tie is a piece of evidence, right?

t_ch: …没错，正是如此！
t_en: ...That's right. That's the one!

t_ch: 花火大人偷走了佐罗的领带，想试试看是否适合自己…谁曾想，佐罗在领带中藏了防盗装置！
t_en: Miss Sparkle stole Lefton's tie to see if it would fit her... But who knew that Lefton had an anti-theft device installed on it!

t_ch: 花火大人刚把领带套在脖子上，它就开始急速收缩，勒死了可怜的大人！她还想将戒指吞下去，保持呼吸通畅，可惜还是…回天乏术！
t_en: After Miss Sparkle affixed the tie to her neck, it rapidly contracted, thereby strangling the poor lady! She tried swallowing her ring to preserve her breathing passage, but unfortunately... she was beyond saving.

t_ch: 真是一场惨剧啊，若不是有您这样明察秋毫的神探，三位嫌疑人一定会蒙受不白之冤！
t_en: What a tragedy. If it weren't for your incisive detective work, the three suspects would all be facing grave injustice!

t_ch: 证据就是这枚戒指，对么？
t_en: This ring is a piece of evidence, right?

t_ch: …没错，正是如此！
t_en: ...That's right. That's the one!

t_ch: 钟珊闯入试衣间，希望讨回自己的戒指，不想花火大人把心一横，直接吞下了它！谁曾想，这一下戒指卡在了喉咙里！
t_en: Zhongshan stormed into the changing room to try and get his ring back — flustering Miss Sparkle, who swallowed it! Who would've guessed that the ring would get stuck in her throat?

t_ch: 钟珊以德报怨，想用借来的领带帮花火大人把戒指挤出来，可惜还是…回天乏术！
t_en: Zhongshan repaid evil with kindness, and tried to borrow a tie to help Miss Sparkle squeeze out the ring. But sadly... she couldn't be saved.

t_ch: 真是一场惨剧啊，若不是有您这样明察秋毫的神探，三位嫌疑人一定会蒙受不白之冤！
t_en: What a tragedy. If it weren't for your incisive detective work, the three suspects would all be facing grave injustice!

t_ch: 证据就是这张板凳，对么？
t_en: This stool is a piece of evidence, right?

t_ch: …没错，正是如此！
t_en: ...That's right. That's the one!

t_ch: 花火大人经商失利，濒临破产。她路过常去的服装店，却偶遇宿敌、一直欺凌的店员、和被自己毁了人生的赌徒……
t_en: Miss Sparkle's business failed, and she was on the brink of bankruptcy. As she passed by her regular fashion store, she ran into her old nemesis, the bullying shopkeeper from the store, and the gambler whose life she'd ruined...

t_ch: 而那三个人看起来竟比自己还要快乐！花火大人的自尊不允许这样的事发生！
t_en: And those three all looked happier than her! Miss Sparkle's ego wouldn't allow that to happen!

t_ch: 于是她偷走佐罗的领带，将钟珊的戒指含入口中，踩在小板凳上，在阿佑的店里悬梁自尽！唯一的目的就是嫁祸三人。
t_en: And so she stole Lefton's tie, placed Zhongshan's ring in her mouth, stepped up onto the stool, and hanged herself from a beam in Wright's shop! And her motive was to try and frame all three of them.

t_ch: 真是一场惨剧啊，若不是有您这样明察秋毫的神探，三位嫌疑人一定会蒙受不白之冤！
t_en: What a tragedy. If it weren't for your incisive detective work, the three suspects would all be facing grave injustice!

t_ch: 嗯？这证据好像没法证明他是凶手吧……
t_en: Hmm? This evidence doesn't really prove that he is the murderer...

t_ch: 竟然不行吗？看来花火小姐比我想象中还要「严谨」一些。
t_en: It doesn't? Looks like Miss Sparkle is more careful than I thought.

t_ch: 再想一想。
t_en: Think again.

t_ch: 别心急，让我再想一想吧。
t_en: Don't worry. Let me think again.

t_ch: 那请您尽快，时间可不等人啊！
t_en: Then please hurry up with it. Time waits for no one!

t_ch: 呃…是不是不太对？这也太简单了吧。虽然第一案也不难，但这个……
t_en: Err... Is something wrong here? This is too easy. The first case wasn't hard, but this one...

t_ch: 如你所想，这些证物指向的结论之间也不存在互斥性，这应当是…花火小姐的又一个恶作剧。
t_en: As you can imagine, the conclusions that the evidence points to are not mutually exclusive. Which means... this is probably another prank from Miss Sparkle.

t_ch: 「侦探助手」先生，请你随意指认一位嫌疑人为凶手。
t_en: Mr. Assistant Detective, please feel free to point out a suspect as the murderer.

t_ch: …哦，原来是这样。警长阁下，凶手是佐罗，证据是领带，对吗？
t_en: ...Ah, I see. Sheriff, the murderer is Lefton. The evidence is the tie, right?

t_ch: 太对了，桑博警探！没想到您也有推理出真相的时候啊！
t_en: Correct, Detective Sampo! I never thought you'd be able to deduce the truth!

t_ch: 警长阁下，凶手是阿佑，证据是板凳，这个对吗？
t_en: Sheriff, the murderer is Wright. The evidence is the stool, right?

t_ch: 对啊！乐子神在上，我怎么从没发现您拥有如此智慧！
t_en: Yes! For The Laughter, how did I fail to realize this formidable intellect of yours!

t_ch: 那…压根就没有凶手？证据就是那个戒指…算了，这我随便乱说的……
t_en: Then... right from the start, there was no murderer? The evidence is the ring... Whatever, I'm just spitballing here...

t_ch: 天啊！副警长阁下，难道他真是天才？如此难以想象的谜底，竟能被他轻易揭开！
t_en: Goodness me! Deputy sheriff, is he really a genius? How could such an unfathomable mystery be solved so easily by him!

t_ch: 看，花火小姐给我们出了一道「所有选项都是正确答案」的谜题。
t_en: Look, Miss Sparkle has given us a puzzle where every single answer is correct.

t_ch: 何必那么认真呢？花火大人是假面愚者，不是假面天才，想要有挑战性的谜题，请出门右转博识学会，我们只负责娱乐性拉满的推理体验！
t_en: Why take it so seriously? Miss Sparkle is a Masked Fool, not a Masked Genius. If it's a challenging puzzle you want, please go out there and turn right until you get to the Intelligentsia Guild. We're only responsible for providing an entertaining detective experience!

t_ch: 总之！鉴于神探黑天鹅成功破解了这起案件，我会将花火大人的下一道谜题分享给二位。
t_en: Anyway! Since our genius detective, Black Swan, has cracked the case, I will now share Miss Sparkle's next puzzle with you.

t_ch: 果然…这样下去根本没完没了，不是办法啊。
t_en: So that's it... This is just going to go on and on forever. We can't do that.

t_ch: 那就祝二位一切顺利！哦对了，如果你们不急着走，我可以给二位讲讲那几个你们没选择的谜底。
t_en: Then I wish you both the best! Oh yes, if you're not in a hurry, I can tell you the answers for some of the outcomes you never chose.

t_ch: 没有奇怪的美梦剧团，也没有满地的证据…咱们终于追上她了。呃，可那是什么东西…一个贝洛伯格的垃圾桶？在匹诺康尼？
t_en: There's no weird Sweet Dreams Troupe, nor is there evidence strewn all over the place... We've finally caught up with her. Um, but what's that thing... A Belobogian trashcan? In Penacony?

t_ch: 嘿！亲爱的忆者，还有寒腿叔叔——你们可算来了！
t_en: Hey! Dear Memokeeper, and Mr. Cold Feet — you made it!

t_ch: 没想到你们竟敢跳过上一道谜题，那可是我的自信之作！算了算了，花火大人不记小人过，来吧，这就是最后一问——
t_en: I didn't expect you to skip the final puzzle. That's on me! Alright, alright, Miss Sparkle cares not for the faults of ants like me. Come then, this is the final question...

t_ch: 请听题！什么生物早上四条腿、中午两条腿、晚上三条腿？
t_en: Listen up! What creature has four legs in the morning, two legs in the afternoon, and three legs at night?

t_ch: 这次怎么不是命案了？
t_en: No murder case this time around?

t_ch: 偶尔也要来点轻松的嘛！来吧，亲爱的，快给出你的回答吧~
t_en: Sometimes it's nice to take things easy! Come on dear, give me your answer then~

t_ch: 人。
t_en: People.

t_ch: 既然是经典的谜题…答案是「人类」，对吗？
t_en: Since it's a classic puzzle, the answer is... "people," right?

t_ch: 卟卟——回答错误！怎么可能是这种无聊的答案！
t_en: Wamp wah — wrong answer. How could it be as boring as that!

t_ch: 虫子。
t_en: Bugs.

t_ch: 嗯，是什么呢…答案是「虫子」，对吗？
t_en: Mmm, what is it then... My answer is "bugs." Is that right?

t_ch: 卟卟——回答错误！你不会以为标新立异就能蒙对吧？
t_en: Wamp wah — wrong answer. That's funky, but a wild guess.

t_ch: 美梦剧团。
t_en: Sweet Dreams Troupe.

t_ch: 既然是在匹诺康尼…答案是「美梦剧团」，对吗？
t_en: Seeing as we're in Penacony... the answer is "Sweet Dreams troupe," right?

t_ch: 卟卟——回答错误！你不会以为标新立异就能蒙对吧？
t_en: Wamp wah — wrong answer. That's funky, but a wild guess.

t_ch: 垃圾桶。
t_en: A trashcan.

t_ch: 既然你以这幅姿态站在我的面前，那我猜…答案是「垃圾桶」，对吗？
t_en: Seeing as you're right here in front of me like this, then I guess... the answer is "a trashcan," right?

t_ch: 卟卟——回答错误！你不会以为标新立异就能蒙对吧？
t_en: Wamp wah — wrong answer. That's funky, but a wild guess.

t_ch: 正确答案是——「花火」！
t_en: The correct answer is: "Sparkle"!

t_ch: 早上困困的爬不起床，中午东奔西跑到处忙，晚上累到趴趴，可还要抬起手刷牙——好可怜的花火呀！
t_en: When sleepy in the morning, she can't get out of bed. She runs about all afternoon in a fluster, so is exhausted by evening — but has to still raise a hand to hold a toothbrush. Poor Sparkle!

t_ch: 所以当然是「早上四条腿，中午两条腿，晚上三条腿」咯～
t_en: So naturally she's got "four legs in the morning, two legs in the afternoon, and three legs at night"~

t_ch: 这都什么乱七八糟的……
t_en: What the heck was that jibber-jabber...

t_ch: 真是可爱的答案呢。
t_en: What a cute answer.

t_ch: 谢谢你的夸奖，亲爱的。不过鉴于你刚才的作弊行为，这次可没那么好糊弄了哦！答错了…就接受惩罚吧！
t_en: Thanks for the compliment, my dear. But you can't try to fool me seeing as you've just tried to cheat! Wrong answer... accept your punishment!

t_ch: 吓、吓死我了！这家伙怎么回事，怎么还突然动手了呢！
t_en: H—Holy smokes! What's up with this guy? He just came out swinging from nowhere!

t_ch: 喔，没声音了？这家伙可算安静了。姐，咱们赶紧打开盖子，把那姑娘揪出来吧……
t_en: Huh, no voices? This guy's pretty quiet. Miss, let's hurry up, flip open the lid and find the girl...

t_ch: ——等等，先别动。
t_en: ...Wait. Don't move.

t_ch: …不对劲。
t_en: ...Something's off.

t_ch: 啊…啊啊啊啊！姐，你…你把花火给打死了！
t_en: Ah... Ahhh! Miss, you... you killed Sparkle!

t_ch: 这、这怎么可能？！匹诺康尼的梦里怎么会死人！流光忆庭的…你做了什么？！
t_en: H—How is this possible? How can people die inside Penacony's dreamscape? In the Garden of Recollection... what did you do!?

t_ch: 是你干的，对不对？在梦里杀人…只有「记忆」的力量能做到这事！你从一开始就计划好了，就因为她捉弄你，跟你开玩笑…你就要杀她灭口！
t_en: You did this, didn't you? Killing people in their dreams... only the Remembrance has the powers to do that! You planned it out all from the start. Because she teased you and was pranking you... you wanted to kill her!

t_ch: 哦…我懂了，你坚持不肯放我走，就是为了拖我下水啊！如果我不答应，下场…下场就会和她一样……
t_en: Oh... I understand. You refused to let me go because you wanted to drag me into all of this! If I don't agree, I'll end up... just like she did...

t_ch: 匹诺康尼的美梦中不存在死亡，这是家族的允诺，也是「同谐」的庇佑。就算是忆者也无法打破这道屏障。
t_en: There is no death inside Penacony's dreamscape. That's the promise of The Family and the blessing of the Harmony. Not even a Memokeeper can break this barrier.

t_ch: 不必如此大费周章，如果你只是想确认这件事…我已经把答案告诉你了，花火小姐。
t_en: There's no need to take such great pains. If you just want confirmation... I've already told you the answer, Miss Sparkle.

t_ch: 花…花火小姐？你在和谁说话呢？
t_en: Miss... Miss Sparkle? Who are you talking to?

t_ch: 你呀。
t_en: You.

t_ch: 「花火伪装成的另一名角色」…不就是你吗，「桑博」先生？
t_en: "Sparkle has disguised herself as another character"... Wouldn't that be you, Mr. Sampo?

t_ch: 这…这你可不能乱说啊！
t_en: This... Don't jump to conclusions!

t_ch: …您要想指认我是花火，可得提出相应的证据才行。哦，证据怕是有点难了，所以…「推理」也可以。
t_en: ...If you want to accuse me of being Sparkle, you have to provide the evidence. Oh, I'm afraid evidence is a little hard to come by... so "deduction" will do.

t_ch: 最后那一题的提示，稍稍有些明显了哦。
t_en: The hint for the final question was a little too obvious.

t_ch: 喔，是「密室杀人事件」？我还以为您不打算理会它了呢。
t_en: Oh, you mean the locked-room murder? I thought you weren't planning on doing that one.

t_ch: 那可是花火小姐的心血，我怎么舍得真的跳过呢？
t_en: All the painstaking effort that Miss Sparkle put into it — how could I ever miss it?

t_ch: 解谜的关键在于侦探提出的两个信息：第一，现场有很多血迹。第二，受害者明显已死去多时。
t_en: The key to solving the puzzle lay in two pieces of information put forward by the detectives: First, the large splatter of blood at the crime scene. Second, the victim had been dead for some time.

t_ch: 以我尚浅的认知，只能得出两个结论：其一是，凶手似乎没有制造「密室杀人」的必要，因为密室总需要一个存在的理由……
t_en: Based on my shallow understanding, I can only draw two conclusions: One is that the murderer did not need to create a locked-room case — because a locked-room always needs a reason to exist...

t_ch: 可按侦探的描述，这间密室并没能延缓目击者发现尸体，现场也没有可供嫁祸的第三者，而满地鲜血也不像是要伪装成自杀……
t_en: But based on the detective's description, this locked-room did not delay the discovery of the body. There was no third party at the crime scene to frame, and the blood everywhere didn't look like a faked suicide...

t_ch: 如此一来，凶手似乎没有制造密室的理由。也有理由推测，如果现场真的是密室——那制造出密室的人，未必是凶手。
t_en: That being so, the murderer seems to have no reason for creating a locked-room. There is also reason to speculate that if the crime scene was a locked-room — then its creator might not necessarily be the murderer.

t_ch: 至于第二个结论就更显而易见了。众人闯入现场时，助手为什么要冲上去止血？这样的专业人士，怎会不惜破坏现场也要抢救一个明显死去多时的人？
t_en: As for my second conclusion, it's even more obvious still. When everyone broke into the scene of the crime, why did the assistant rush off to staunch the blood? A trained professional was somehow happily destroying a crime scene just to save someone who's obviously been dead a while?

t_ch: 答案很简单——因为「侦探助手」就是凶手。
t_en: The answer is simple: It's because the assistant detective is the murderer.

t_ch: 当时走得匆忙，没有听完整个谜面，我姑且做出如下推测：凶手在前夜杀害了死者，但并没有反锁房门。
t_en: At the time I was in such a hurry that I didn't hear the whole mystery. I tentatively speculated that the murderer had killed the victim the night before, but hadn't locked the door from the inside.

t_ch: 因此当他得知这一信息时，就意识到是一息尚存的死者自己制造了密室。
t_en: And so when he learned this piece of information, he realized that the victim had created their own locked-room mystery.

t_ch: 为此，他必须第一时间赶到现场，确认死者是否留下了对自己不利的证据。而从他的行为来看，应该和那块画布有关吧，也许死亡讯息就在其上。
t_en: That's why he had to rush to the crime scene as soon as possible, to confirm whether or not the victim left any evidence implicating him. And judging by his behavior, it probably had something to do with that canvas, like some final words.

t_ch: 当然，这只是一个简单的思维游戏。谜底是什么并不重要，花火小姐只是为了暗示我一件事——凶手是「侦探助手」……
t_en: Of course, this is just a simple mind game. The answer to the puzzle isn't important. Miss Sparkle just wanted to pass on one hint to me: That the murderer is the assistant detective...

t_ch: …也就是你，「桑博」先生。尽管我一直在使用这个称呼——但它最早是你提出的。
t_en: ...which means it's you, Mr. Sampo. I've been using this term of address — but it was you who first came up with it.

t_ch: 有没有人说过，比起忆者，你更像是一位侦探。
t_en: Has anyone ever told you that you're more like a detective than a Memokeeper?

t_ch: 在我看来，这二者并无本质区别。循着水面上的细微波纹，使用一些能力和技巧，不断深潜，触及人心中最深的秘密，将其打捞，悉心保护……
t_en: In my opinion, there is no difference between the two. Follow ripples along the surface of the water. Use some abilities and tricks to keep diving deeper, and touch that secret deep inside people's hearts. Salvage it, and protect them carefully...

t_ch: 真相不会说谎，「记忆」也不会。
t_en: The truth does not lie. Neither do memories.

t_ch: 非常精彩的推理，已经无限接近真相了…只不过，我这还有另一个谜底，您想听听看吗？
t_en: Exquisite reasoning, and very close to the truth... But I have another answer. Would you like to hear it?

t_ch: 另一个谜底？
t_en: Another answer?

t_ch: 大艺术家花火反锁上卧室的房门，用红色颜料制造出了「到处都是血」的假象。而那位第一个冲进室内，并证明她已死亡的侦探助手……
t_en: The great artist Sparkle locked herself away in her bedroom, using red paint to create the semblance of spattered blood everywhere. And that assistant detective who was first on the scene who confirmed her death...

t_ch: …并不是凶手，而是协助她诈死的「共犯」。
t_en: ...was not the murderer, but rather an accomplice who assisted her in faking her own death.

t_ch: 遗憾，遗憾。亲爱的，你真的只差一点点点点就答对了。可惜，正确答案是——
t_en: What a pity. Such a pity. My dear, you were so-so-so-SO close to the right answer. Unfortunately, the right answer is...

t_ch: ——「花火大人的尸体」。
t_en: ...Miss Sparkle's body.

t_ch: 「花火」，伪装成了「花火的尸体」。
t_en: Sparkle was disguised as Sparkle's body.

t_ch: 黑天鹅小姐，桑博先生，你们终于来了！我是匹诺康尼的「名侦探」，旁边这位是我的「侦探助手」。
t_en: Miss Black Swan, Mr. Sampo: You're finally here! I am the famed detective of Penacony, and next to me here is my assistant detective.

t_ch: 这里竟发生了令我们都感到棘手的<color=#dbc291ff>密室杀人事件</color>！
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>A murder in a locked room</color> has left us all stumped!

t_ch: 不出所料，又换设定了……
t_en: As expected, another change in setting...

t_ch: 昨日，我和助手前来拜访大艺术家花火大人，并在她家中借宿一晚。可第二天早上，花火大人却迟迟没有从卧室中出来。
t_en: Yesterday, my assistant and I were visiting the famed artist Miss Sparkle, and we stayed the night. But this morning, Miss Sparkle didn't come out of her bedroom.

t_ch: 管家钟珊说，房门从里面反锁了，他本不该打搅…可花火大人从不会这么晚起床，而且无论他怎么敲门，里面也没有任何回应。
t_en: The butler, Zhongshan, said that the door was locked from inside, so he ought not to have disturbed her... But Miss Sparkle had never slept in so late, and no matter how hard he knocked, there was no answer from inside.

t_ch: 我一听就知道，这肯定是出事了！于是当机立断，撞破大门冲进了房间……
t_en: As soon as I heard, I knew something must have happened! So I acted decisively and broke the door down to get inside...

t_ch: 房间里到处都是血啊！「花火大人的尸体」就躺在正中央，面色白得像雪，一看就知道<color=#dbc291ff>死去多时</color>了……
t_en: The room was covered in spattered blood! Miss Sparkle's body was lying right in the middle, her face as white as snow. One look told me she'd been <color=#dbc291ff>dead for some time</color>...

t_ch: 我赶忙抓起一旁的画布为花火大人止血，可…哎，可怜的花火大人，还是永远离开了我们！
t_en: Thinking quickly, I grabbed the artist's canvas to staunch Miss Sparkle's blood, but... Alas, poor Miss Sparkle. She's left us now, forever!

t_ch: 当时在现场的，除了我俩和管家钟珊，还有厨师佐罗和司机阿佑…哎，黑天鹅小姐，你要做什么？
t_en: Besides us and Zhongshan the butler, Lefton the cook and Wright the driver were also at the scene of the crime... What would you like to do?

t_ch: 想必现在…花火小姐正在精心布置下一个「案发现场」吧？
t_en: Presumably... Miss Sparkle is now carefully arranging the next crime scene, right?

t_ch: 不如略过那些复杂的过程，和我来一场敞开心扉的交流呢？可能会有点痒哦。
t_en: Why don't you skip all this rigmarole and just have a heart-to-heart conversation with me? It might tickle a little.

t_ch: 哎…等等…等等！
t_en: Wait up... wait up!

t_ch: 嗯…诚实的孩子。我知道你在哪儿了，花火小姐。
t_en: Hmmm... honest kid. I know where you are, Miss Sparkle.

t_ch: 你怎么作弊！
t_en: No cheating!

t_ch: 怎么了，「侦探助手」先生？你好像很心急的样子。
t_en: Huh, Mr. Assistant Detective? You seem to be getting anxious?

t_ch: 啊，不…我，这…哈哈，这密室杀人事件有点意思，我还想猜猜看呢……
t_en: Ah, no... I, it's... Haha, this locked-room murder case is pretty fun. I wanna guess who it was...

t_ch: 走吧——不能让女孩子久等呀。
t_en: Let's go — can't keep a girl waiting, can we?

t_ch: 喂！黑天鹅小姐，等一等，等一等！花火大人还有最后一件事要宣布——
t_en: Hey! Miss Black Swan, wait up. Wait up! Miss Sparkle has one final message to announce...

t_ch: 锵锵——现在就是一出戏剧最令人激动也最浪漫的时刻：挑战观众环节！
t_en: Ding-a-ling~ Now comes the most exciting and romantic moment in any drama: The audience challenge!

t_ch: 众所周知，可怕的假面愚者花火可以变化为其他人的样貌。而在这出剧目中，大反派花火已将自己伪装成了另一名角色。
t_en: Everyone knows that the mighty Masked Fool Sparkle can change her appearance to look like others. And in this drama, the villain, Sparkle, has disguised herself as another character.

t_ch: 那么请问——这名角色究竟是谁呢！
t_en: So then, I put to you: Which character is it!

t_ch: 另外，那位忆者不想挑战的密室杀人事件，若诸位有兴趣也可试着推理一番。花火好不容易想出来的谜题，可别浪费了！
t_en: Also, if you're interested in the locked-room case that the Memokeeper ignored, you can try out your powers of deduction. I put lots into coming up with the puzzle, so don't let it go to waste!

t_ch: 以上，便是「假面天才」花火大人的挑战书！所有线索已经备齐——祝君狩猎愉快！
t_en: Thus the Masked Genius Miss Sparkle has thrown down the gauntlet! All the clues are in motion — happy hunting!

t_ch: 二位还有什么事吗？
t_en: Do you two need anything else?

t_ch: 想再确认下案情。
t_en: Just want to confirm case details again.

t_ch: 请你再为我们说明下案发经过吧。
t_en: Please can you explain to us again the details of the case?

t_ch: 没问题。你想问哪件事？
t_en: No problem. What would you like to know about?

t_ch: 关于受害者…
t_en: Regarding the victim...

t_ch: 是位星际和平公司的职员，叫沙马里。听目击者说，有个人高马大的壮汉，穿得宽袍大袖，和沙马里擦肩而过时突然一锤子砸了下去…
t_en: An IPC employee named Shamari. Eyewitnesses reported that a tall, strong man wearing a large robe with long sleeves walked right past Shamari, then suddenly assaulted him with a hammer...

t_ch: 沙马里当场就不行了，一下从梦里惊醒了过来。那凶手也转眼消失不见。
t_en: Shamari collapsed on the spot and woke up from the dream. The murderer vanished in a flash.

t_ch: 关于现场的美梦剧团…
t_en: Regarding the Sweet Dreams Troupe present at the scene of the crime...

t_ch: 似乎是袭击者从别处偷来的美梦剧团，故意放在这儿的…真气人，简直就是不把公共秩序放在眼里。
t_en: Seems like the assailant stole the troupe from elsewhere and deliberately placed them here. What nerve... They don't even have the slightest consideration for public order.

t_ch: 那俩东西一直不肯说话，不知道二位能否撬开它们的嘴。
t_en: Those two have clammed up. I wonder if you two could help pry open their mouths.

t_ch: 关于现场的证物…
t_en: Regarding evidence at the scene of the crime...

t_ch: 因为是突发事件，没什么有用的线索，只有袭击者使用的凶器，和受害者的证件……
t_en: This is a developing case, so there aren't many clues yet. All we have to go by are the weapon used by the assailant and the victim's ID...

t_ch: 那人没带走凶器，大概是在向我们挑衅吧…太可恨了。
t_en: They didn't even take the weapon with them. Sounds like they're trying to provoke us... Despicable.

t_ch: 没有其他问题了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 谢谢，我没有其他问题了。
t_en: Thank you. I don't have any further questions.

t_ch: 不用谢，二位想知道什么尽管开口。
t_en: You're welcome. If there's anything you'd like to know, please ask away.

t_ch: 没有其他问题了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 谢谢，我没有其他问题了。
t_en: Thank you. I don't have any further questions.

t_ch: 不用谢，二位想知道什么尽管开口。
t_en: You're welcome. If there's anything you'd like to know, please ask away.

t_ch: 接下来…来会会这两个小家伙吧。
t_en: Next up... let's interview those two over there.

t_ch: 那姑娘说它们一直保持沉默，但如果对你有反应，就代表它们真是花火布置的谜题了。
t_en: That girl said they're always silent, but if they gave you a reaction, it means they're actually one of Sparkle's puzzles.

t_ch: 两名猎犬家系的保镖保护着犯罪现场，确保不会有任何无关人员靠近。
t_en: The two Bloodhound guards are protecting the crime scene, ensuring no one unrelated to the matter is able to get near.

t_ch: 他们不会突然意识到，咱们根本不是什么「侦查人员」吧……
t_en: You don't think they'll realize we aren't actually detectives, do you?

t_ch: 当然不会。请多信任我一点。
t_en: No way. Please put more trust in me.

t_ch: 请黑天鹅大人开始调查吧！案发现场留下了许多线索，一定能帮助您锁定真凶。
t_en: Please begin your investigation, Lady Black Swan! There are lots of clues at the crime scene that are sure to help you apprehend the real culprit.

t_ch: 请问您还有什么问题吗？
t_en: Have you got any more questions?

t_ch: 想再听听嫌疑人信息。
t_en: I'd like to hear about the suspects one more time.

t_ch: 佐罗是花火大人部下悍将，在对抗山贼时，奋勇抗敌，<color=#dbc291ff>右手不幸被斩断了</color>。现在正在练习用左手写字和拿筷子，真是可怜啊。
t_en: Lefton is one of Miss Sparkle's chief lieutenants, whose <color=#dbc291ff>right hand was unfortunately severed</color> while he was valiantly fighting off the Annihilation Gang. He's now learning how to write and hold a fork with his left hand — how tough that must be!

t_ch: 钟珊这人胆子比较小，遇见山贼转身就跑，结果撞到了山岩上，<color=#dbc291ff>整张脸都破相了</color>。可惜可惜，以前他可是城中难得一见的美男子啊。
t_en: Then there's Zhongshan — this guy's a coward who ran away as soon as the group spotted some bandits along the road. He ended up smashing into the side of a cliff and <color=#dbc291ff>getting his face disfigured</color>. It's a real shame, as he used to be one of the rare handsome men left around town.

t_ch: 最后是阿佑，这家伙贪得无厌，好不容易逃出生天，竟打算私吞剩下的货物，谎称是被山贼全部抢走了…佐罗和钟珊气不过，就<color=#dbc291ff>把他的腿打断了</color>。
t_en: Last of all comes Wright. This guy was so insatiably greedy that he still came back to try and embezzle the rest of the supplies after they narrowly escaped with their lives, lying about how the gang had taken off with everything... Lefton and Zhongshan were so incensed that they <color=#dbc291ff>broke his legs</color>.

t_ch: 以上就是三位嫌疑人全部的信息了。
t_en: That's all the intel we have on the three suspects.

t_ch: 没什么问题。
t_en: No questions.

t_ch: 对于案件我没有疑问，只是有些好奇…花火是怎么操纵你的？
t_en: I have no questions about the case, but I'm just curious... How did Sparkle manipulate you?

t_ch: 我只是一介捕快，不清楚和本案无关的事情！
t_en: I'm just a lowly bailiff. I don't know anything except for this 'ere case!

t_ch: 看，这有张证件——「人才激励部，沙马里」…是受害者的东西？
t_en: Look, an ID card: "Talent Motivation Department: Shamari"... Is this the victim's?

t_ch: 随着沙马里先生的惊醒，他的随身物品本应一并从梦境中消失才对。这张证件应该是花火小姐放置的，为了证明她确实袭击过某个人。
t_en: Mr. Shamari's belongings should have disappeared alongside him as he woke from the Dreamscape. The fact this ID is here means Miss Sparkle placed it here on purpose to prove that she definitely attacked someone.

t_ch: 我的个乖乖，这锤子分量可不轻啊…应该就是「花火大人遇害案」的凶器了。
t_en: Holy moly, this hammer is super heavy... This must be the murder weapon for the Sparkle Murder Case.

t_ch: 花火小姐也是用它袭击了沙马里先生吧。
t_en: Miss Sparkle also used it to attack Mr. Shamari.

t_ch: 这东西我两只手拎着都费劲，真不知道花火是怎么用它来打人的……
t_en: I'm barely able to lift this using both hands. I have no idea how Sparkle could ever use this to hit a target...

t_ch: 所以，凶手究竟是谁？
t_en: So who exactly is the murderer?

t_ch: 「佐罗」，这家伙的<color=#dbc291ff>手好像断了</color>……
t_en: Lefton. That guy <color=#dbc291ff>lost his hand</color>...

t_ch: 根据捕快的供词，佐罗的惯用手受了重伤，甚至必须学习用左手生活。挥舞那柄巨型铁锤行凶…对他应该相对困难，甚至是不可能的事吧。
t_en: Based on the bailiff's statement, Lefton's dominant hand was seriously injured to the point where he had to learn how to live using only his left hand. Swinging that giant sledgehammer to murder someone... that must be hard for him. Probably impossible.

t_ch: 佐罗的嫌疑可以下降了。
t_en: Lefton's suspicion level can be downgraded.

t_ch: 呃…是这样吗，感觉证据有点对不上号啊？要不，再想想？
t_en: Er... is that so? I feel like the evidence doesn't really match? How about you think it over again?

t_ch: 所以，凶手究竟是谁？
t_en: So who exactly is the murderer?

t_ch: 记得「钟珊」是<color=#dbc291ff>把脸摔破相了</color>，没错吧？
t_en: Remember Zhongshan's face was <color=#dbc291ff>disfigured</color> after the incident at the cliff, right?

t_ch: 可怜的人，脸上受了重伤，就算能保住美貌，最近也不得不缠着纱布出行吧。如此一来，他是无法通过金槌府邸的人脸识别系统的。
t_en: Poor guy. He was seriously injured and was all wrapped up in bandages to keep his good looks safe. If that's the case, then he couldn't have made it through the Goldhammer residence's facial recognition system.

t_ch: 如此看来，钟珊不会是凶手。
t_en: That would suggest that Zhongshan is not the murderer.

t_ch: 呃…是这样吗，感觉证据有点对不上号啊？要不，再想想？
t_en: Er... is that so? I feel like the evidence doesn't really match? How about you think it over again?

t_ch: 这么看来，凶手也只能是阿佑了——有点简单啊。咱们把答案告诉捕头，看看它是什么反应吧。
t_en: By process of elimination, it seems that only Wright could be the murderer. That was easy. Let's tell the constable our answer and see what he thinks.

t_ch: 所以，凶手究竟是谁？
t_en: So who exactly is the murderer?

t_ch: 这人我印象很深，「阿佑」的<color=#dbc291ff>腿断了</color>。
t_en: I remember this one best. Wright has a <color=#dbc291ff>broken leg</color>.

t_ch: 呃…是这样吗，感觉证据有点对不上号啊？要不，再想想？
t_en: Er... is that so? I feel like the evidence doesn't really match? How about you think it over again?

t_ch: 捕头和捕快成了两具颓然的死物。看来，它们已经完成任务了。
t_en: At this moment, the constable and his bailiff lay lifelessly, mere husks of their former selves. It seems they have already completed their mission.

t_ch: 所以，你究竟为什么对花火的挑战这么上心？还是说…你关心的是她这个人？
t_en: So, why are you so focused on Sparkle's challenge? Or should I ask... are you more focused on Sparkle herself?

t_ch: 我说过，这只是出于一些…小小的好胜心。
t_en: I told you, it's purely out of a spirit of competitiveness.

t_ch: 以及，身为忆者，我也要履行自己的职责，去收获一些「足够有趣」的记忆。
t_en: And, as a Memokeeper, I also have to fulfill my responsibilities and harvest some interesting memories.

t_ch: 如何，黑天鹅警探，你找出凶手是谁了吗？
t_en: How's it going, Detective Black Swan? Have you found out who the killer is?
